data.title: EchoStar GAAP EPS of -$0.71 beats by $0.09, revenue of $3.87B in-line
data.description:
• EchoStar press release (NASDAQ:SATS): Q1 GAAP EPS of -$0.71 beats by $0.09.
  • Revenue of $3.87B (-3.5% Y/Y) in-line.
  • Net subscriber growth (+150K), improved churn (7.2% year-over-year), filed Federal Communication Commission  certification of more than 24,000 5G constructed "on-air" sites one month ahead of schedule
  • Pay TV: Lowest DISH TV churn (1.36%) in over a decade (excluding the pandemic), growth in average revenue per user (+3% year-over-year)
  • Broadband & Satellite Services: Enhanced in-flight offerings through universal Ka- and Ku-bands, new international contracts for managed network services, expanded enterprise backlog (+5% year-over-year)

data.title: Essent Group GAAP EPS of $1.69 beats by $0.04, revenue of $317.56M beats by $6.77M
data.description:
• Essent Group press release (NYSE:ESNT): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.69 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $317.56M (+6.4% Y/Y) beats by $6.77M.
  • New insurance written for the first quarter of 2025 was $9.9 billion, compared to $12.2 billion in the fourth quarter of 2024 and $8.3 billion in the first quarter of 2024.
  • Insurance in force as of March 31, 2025 was $244.7 billion, compared to $243.6 billion as of December 31, 2024 and $238.5 billion as of March 31, 2024.

data.title: Italy's Moltiply sues Google in €2.97B lawsuit over alleged market dominance
data.description:
Italy's Moltiply Group said its unit was suing Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (NASDAQ:GOOG) Google and is seeking damages of €2.97B (about $3.34B) for alleged abuse of its dominant market position as previously recognized by the EU's Court of Justice.

Moltiply said on Friday that its subsidiary 7Pixel, which operates the price comparison service Trovaprezzi.it, recently notified several "Google Group entities of a follow-on claim for damages for the abuse of dominance that favored Google Shopping between 2010 and 2017, as finally determined by the European Court of Justice in September 2024."

Moltiply noted that the amount of damages estimated by the experts commissioned by 7Pixel, including structural effects of the abuse and interest, is equal to €2.97B.

Google did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

The claim was filed at a Milan court, Reuters reported, citing Italian daily Corriere della Sera, which did not give further details.

Last year, in September, the EU's top court upheld a fine of €2.42B imposed on Google for abusing its dominant position by favoring its comparison shopping service, Google Shopping, in search results. In 2017, the Commission accused Google of favoring Google Shopping, thereby disadvantaging competitors.

data.title: Ziprecruiter signals year-over-year growth potential in Q4 2025 amid cautious optimism
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ZipRecruiter (ZIP) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ian Siegel highlighted a sequential increase of 10% in quarterly paid employers, the highest Q4 to Q1 growth since 2021, despite a slight 1% sequential revenue decline to $110 million. He noted that employer hiring activity remains consistent, though macroeconomic uncertainty persists.
  • Siegel expressed confidence in achieving year-over-year revenue growth by Q4 2025, supported by investments in long-term strategic initiatives and a robust balance sheet. The company aims to maintain adjusted EBITDA margins in the mid-single digits for the year.
  • President David Travers emphasized advancements in the company’s platform, including 180 applicant tracking system integrations and the enhancement of the next-generation resume database. Enterprise employers using the database reported a 9% increase in resume unlock rates.
  • CFO Tim Yarborough reported a net loss of $12.8 million for Q1 2025, compared to $6.5 million in Q1 2024. Adjusted EBITDA margin stood at 5%, in line with guidance, reflecting higher marketing and personnel expenses.

Outlook

  • Management provided Q2 2025 revenue guidance of $111 million at the midpoint, a 1% increase quarter-over-quarter, while forecasting a 6% adjusted EBITDA margin.
  • The company reiterated its belief in achieving year-over-year revenue growth by Q4 2025, contingent upon stable hiring trends.
  • Despite macroeconomic uncertainty, management emphasized its ability to adjust marketing and operational investments dynamically to align with hiring demand.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q1 2025 was $110 million, down 10% year-over-year and 1% quarter-over-quarter. This sequential decline aligns with typical seasonal patterns.
  • Quarterly paid employers increased by 10% sequentially to 63,000, the largest Q4 to Q1 growth since 2021. However, this figure represents an 11% decline year-over-year.
  • Revenue per paid employer was $1,734, up 2% year-over-year but down 10% sequentially, reflecting seasonal employer activity shifts.
  • The company’s cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $468 million as of March 31, 2025.

Q&A

  • Ralph Schackart, William Blair: Asked about geographic and vertical differences in employer hiring trends. CEO Siegel responded that hiring activity remains consistent across geographies and verticals, with no significant pullbacks observed.
  • Josh Chan, UBS: Questioned the company’s approach to managing investment amid macroeconomic uncertainty. CFO Yarborough explained ZipRecruiter’s flexible operational model, allowing rapid adjustments to marketing spend based on demand.
  • Trevor Young, Barclays: Inquired about employer hesitancy in hiring practices. Travers noted that employers are taking slightly longer to hire but remain consistent in their approach, correlating more with quit rates than recent macroeconomic shifts.
  • Eric Sheridan, Goldman Sachs: Asked about competitive landscape and AI initiatives. Siegel emphasized ZipRecruiter’s focus on gaining job seeker traffic share and detailed ongoing AI-driven enhancements to improve matching and engagement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, acknowledging steady hiring activity but probing management on macroeconomic risks and flexibility in investment strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, repeatedly emphasizing the company’s ability to adapt to varying market conditions and highlighting product advancements as drivers of future growth.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone reflected increased confidence in achieving year-over-year growth by Q4 2025, despite unchanged macroeconomic uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management reaffirmed its Q4 2025 growth outlook, consistent with statements from the previous quarter, while maintaining cautious optimism regarding macroeconomic trends.
  • Sequential growth in quarterly paid employers contrasted with declines in prior years, signaling improved seasonality and employer engagement.
  • Analysts continued to focus on hiring demand stability and the company’s ability to adjust investments dynamically, similar to concerns raised in Q4 2024.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified macroeconomic uncertainty as a key challenge but noted stable employer hiring activity thus far in 2025.
  • The company’s ability to achieve Q4 2025 revenue growth depends on consistent hiring trends amidst a volatile economic backdrop.
  • Analysts raised concerns about employer hesitancy and the potential for broader market deterioration affecting hiring demand.

Final Takeaway

ZipRecruiter’s Q1 2025 results showcased resilience amid macroeconomic uncertainty, with sequential growth in paid employers and steady hiring activity. Management remains optimistic about achieving year-over-year revenue growth by Q4 2025, supported by ongoing product advancements and a flexible operational model. However, macroeconomic risks and employer hesitancy remain key concerns, emphasizing the importance of adaptability in investment and strategic planning.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
